testcase.name: test_key_is_str

failure.message:
assert b"+\xb8\rS{\x...e9{\xf5'\xa2[" == b'K7gNU3sdo-O...2ol_pe_Unols='
At index 0 diff: b'+' != b'K' Use -v to get more diff

testcase.failure:
self = <encrypt_decrypt_fields.tests.test_crypto.TestCrypt object at 0x1077bc410>
def test_key_is_str(self): crypto = Crypto("secret") key = crypto.get_key()
assert isinstance(key, bytes) > assert key == b"K7gNU3sdo-OL0wNhqoVWhr3g6s1xYv72ol_pe_Unols=" E assert b"+\xb8\rS{\x...e9{\xf5'\xa2[" == b'K7gNU3sdo-O...2ol_pe_Unols=' E E At index 0 diff: b'+' != b'K' E Use -v to get more diff
encrypt_decrypt_fields/tests/test_crypto.py:12: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_passed_string_value

failure.message: binascii.Error: Invalid base64-encoded string: number of data characters (97) cannot be 1 more than a multiple of 4

testcase.failure:
self = <encrypt_decrypt_fields.tests.test_django_field.TestDjangoEncryptField object at 0x10780cdd0> connection = <Mock id='4420916880'>
def test_passed_string_value(self, connection): connection.Database.Binary.return_value = ( b"gAAAAABg1vz2rLbNvztnC8p2hQbBGzrnyuGN772bEY5" b"w9c1s6fVmqZm4bkjJPfr2PAWZZ3twsLcajxK5y6g-KpjjeMO5luh0fg==" ) value = self.FIELD.get_db_prep_value("password", connection)
connection.Database.Binary.assert_called_once() assert not isinstance(value, str) > assert Crypto("secret").decrypt_token(value) == "password" ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
encrypt_decrypt_fields/tests/test_django_field.py:41: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ encrypt_decrypt_fields/services/crypto.py:27: in decrypt_token raw = base64.b64decode(token) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
s = b'gAAAAABg1vz2rLbNvztnC8p2hQbBGzrnyuGN772bEY5w9c1s6fVmqZm4bkjJPfr2PAWZZ3twsLcajxK5y6g-KpjjeMO5luh0fg==' altchars = None, validate = False
def b64decode(s, altchars=None, validate=False): """Decode the Base64 encoded bytes-like object or ASCII string s.
Optional altchars must be a bytes-like object or ASCII string of length 2 which specifies the alternative alphabet used instead of the '+' and '/' characters.
The result is returned as a bytes object. A binascii.Error is raised if s is incorrectly padded.
If validate is False (the default), characters that are neither in the normal base-64 alphabet nor the alternative alphabet are discarded prior to the padding check. If validate is True, these non-alphabet characters in the input result in a binascii.Error. For more information about the strict base64 check, see:
https://docs.python.org/3.11/library/binascii.html#binascii.a2b_base64 """ s = _bytes_from_decode_data(s) if altchars is not None: altchars = _bytes_from_decode_data(altchars) assert len(altchars) == 2, repr(altchars) s = s.translate(bytes.maketrans(altchars, b'+/')) > return binascii.a2b_base64(s, strict_mode=validate) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E binascii.Error: Invalid base64-encoded string: number of data characters (97) cannot be 1 more than a multiple of 4
/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/base64.py:88: Error
